directives.name: COMPILE
directives.description: Compiler directive that includes a block of source code in compilation. Expression parameter is optional - if omitted, always compiles code. If provided (typically an EQUATE), compiles code conditionally when expression is TRUE. Block ends at the line containing the terminator string.

directives.name: EQUATE
directives.description: Assigns a label to a constant value, picture token, data type, or another label. Creates compile-time constant that uses no runtime memory. Used for readability and maintainability (e.g., TRUE EQUATE(1), PI EQUATE(3.1415927)). Cannot equate to itself. In ITEMIZE structure, value parameter is optional.

directives.name: INCLUDE
directives.description: Compiler directive that includes source code from another file. Section parameter is optional - if omitted, includes entire file. If provided, includes only the specified SECTION. Extension .CLW assumed if omitted.

directives.name: ITEMIZE
directives.description: Declares an enumerated data structure. Each EQUATE inside auto-increments by 1 from the previous (or from seed). Equates are referenced with the PRE prefix or the ITEMIZE label. If no value and no seed, the first equate defaults to 1.

directives.name: MEMBER
directives.description: Compiler directive that identifies this source file as a MEMBER module. Program parameter is optional - omit for single-program applications, specify for multi-program solutions. Must appear at top of file before any MAP.

directives.name: OMIT
directives.description: Compiler directive that omits a block of source code from compilation. Expression parameter is optional - if omitted, always omits code. If provided (typically an EQUATE), omits code conditionally when expression is TRUE. Block ends at the line containing the terminator string.

directives.name: PRAGMA
directives.description: Compiler directive that sends project system statements from source code. Format: PRAGMA('project(statement)'), PRAGMA('compile(options)'), or PRAGMA('link(options)'). Dynamically adds pragma settings to project. Commonly used to control compilation, linking, and optimization settings from within code.

directives.name: SECTION
directives.description: Identifies the beginning of a named block of executable source code or data declarations. The string parameter is used as the optional section parameter of the INCLUDE directive to include a specific block from a file. A SECTION ends at the next SECTION or end of file.
